title: senecio spathulatus
intro: Senecio spathulatus, also known as blue chalk fingers, is a succulent plant species native to South Africa. This plant contains pyrrolizidine alkaloids, which have been found to have medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma due to its expectorant properties. The health benefits of Senecio spathulatus include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of coughs and colds. Herbal preparations often involve drying and crushing the leaves to be used in teas, infusions, or tinctures, although its use is not without controversy due to the potential toxicity of its alkaloids.
